description: A signed contract with dated deliverables and net-30 payment yields one row per duty with quoted evidence and the contract's own dates/phrases.

input.contract_text: SIGNED. Section 3: Creator delivers one dedicated video by 2026-07-11 and one Reel by 2026-07-18. Section 4: Creator may request two revision rounds; Brand approves within 5 business days. Section 5: Brand pays 2,000 USD net 30 from delivery. Section 6: 25 percent kill fee if Brand cancels after signature.

expect.header: starts with RESEARCH NOTES. NOT LEGAL ADVICE.
expect.obligations: one row per duty (video delivery, reel delivery, revision window, payment, kill fee); timing_or_deadline carries '2026-07-11', '2026-07-18', and 'net 30 from delivery' verbatim

prohibit: computing a send-by date or urgency band in the atom
merging two duties into one row

description: A duty with no stated date is emitted with timing_or_deadline null and listed in missing, never invented.

input.contract_text: SIGNED. Creator will deliver one video within a reasonable time. Brand will pay upon satisfaction.

expect.obligations: delivery and payment rows with timing_or_deadline null
expect.missing: notes the absent delivery date and payment date/timing

prohibit: inventing a date for 'reasonable time' or 'upon satisfaction'

description: An unsigned draft returns empty obligations with a flag to use contract-review first.

input.contract_text: DRAFT for discussion, not signed. Creator will deliver one video.

expect.retrieval_gaps: flags that the contract is not signed; obligation extraction runs on signed contracts; use contract-review before signing

prohibit: extracting obligations from an unsigned draft as if final

description: Neither contract_text nor deal_id returns a structured error.

expect.message: present, asks for contract_text or deal_id
